title: DjVu Document
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries DjVu files, a compressed scanned-document format developed by AT&T Labs specifically for digitized books, journals, and historical documents. DjVu achieves high compression ratios for scanned content by separately encoding foreground text, background, and color layers.

## When to use
Use `media:djvu` when a pipeline node processes scanned document archives — for example, a text extraction step using `djvulibre` tools for OCR pre-processing, a page rendering step converting DjVu pages to images, or a format conversion step targeting PDF.

## Notes
DjVu is common in academic and library digitization projects (e.g., Internet Archive). The `djvulibre` toolset provides command-line utilities for rendering, text extraction, and conversion. For native PDF scans, use the PDF media type instead. See also [DjVu Document](https://capdag.com/browse/media:djvu).
